summary: Full-stack testing of CryptoAI MVP. 16/16 backend pytest cases pass (after CoinGecko rate-limit recovery). Frontend login -> dashboard, portfolio (all 4 tabs), and alerts (create/check/delete + signal alert) all work end-to-end. Gemini explanation returns real text. ML prediction, indicators, signal badge, range buttons, and coin row switch all functional.

minor.issue: CoinGecko free tier rate-limits aggressively -> backend returns 502 'CoinGecko error: 429' under burst load. Cache TTL helps but only 30-120s. Consider exponential backoff, longer TTL, or stale-while-revalidate. Currently expected per task brief.

issues: Recharts console warning: 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on initial render before data arrives. Cosmetic only - chart renders correctly once data loads.

action_items: Optional: Increase CoinGecko cache TTL or implement stale-while-revalidate to soften 429s for end users.
Optional: Guard recharts ResponsiveContainer with min height/width or render chart only after data is loaded to remove console warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 629 lines - consider splitting into routers (auth, market, analysis, portfolio, alerts) for maintainability.

CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False - OK for header-Bearer auth, but the cookie auth path (httpOnly access_token) won't work cross-origin from the deployed frontend until origins are explicit and credentials=true.
_cg_get cache is process-local dict (not shared, no eviction). Fine for MVP, replace with TTLCache or Redis if scaled.
ml_predict refits RandomForest on every request - acceptable for MVP but should be cached per coin/window.
/analysis/explain falls back silently to a non-Gemini text if Gemini errors - good resilience but should log/expose source flag (ai vs heuristic).
seed_data_creation: Created/cleaned TEST_ users, holdings, paper trades, and alerts during tests; all cleaned up via DELETE endpoints.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints and pages verified. CoinGecko 502s are transient rate-limits, not real bugs - rerun pytest after a 60-90s wait if it fails. Auth uses both httpOnly cookie AND Bearer token; frontend uses Bearer (localStorage 'ca_token'). Gemini returns real explanations.
rca of the issue: N/A - no real bugs. The only failures observed were transient 502s from CoinGecko free-tier 429 rate limiting (mitigation: backoff/longer cache).
